rationale: X's review directly reconstructs the ground-truth reviewer's core insight — that submit_initial and submit_update both reject list prompts for diffusion, so gating the unwrap on already_submitted was wrong — with precise file:line grounding, matching the single substantive human comment almost exactly. Y verifies similar facts but never articulates that both-paths-reject-list reasoning, instead pivoting to a REQUEST CHANGES verdict over missing tests (a reasonable but secondary concern, and contradicted by two clean LGTM approvals), and its output leaks raw agent-run artifacts (state_updates, dropped-candidate notes, 'budget exhausted' hedges) that bury the actionable findings and hurt clarity. X is also internally consistent and delivered as a clean, mergeable-review format, whereas Y reads like an unpolished log dump.
